title: Eriolaena rigida
taxonomy: Eriolaena rigida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a part of the genus Eriolaena, which consists of several species of plants native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions. Eriolaena rigida is a type of euphorb, a group of plants characterized by their small, often globular fruits and milky latex. The plant's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its rigid stems and distinctive leaf morphology. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Eriolaena rigida shares similarities with other euphorb species, including Cassytha and Pedilanthus.
uses: Eriolaena rigida is reported to have various medicinal properties, including antimalarial and antibacterial activities. The plant has been traditionally used in Indian medicine to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Its extracts are believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases. Additionally, Eriolaena rigida has been studied for its potential in treating various types of infections, including those caused by bacteria and fungi. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Eriolaena rigida as a medicinal plant.

intro: Eriolaena rigida is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the "Indian coral tree" due to its vibrant, coral-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin disorders, thanks to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties.
